[sonnet-collision-13|skeptic:accept] field/object conflation: Q4874481 'Bayesian statistics' is P31 Q11862829 (academic discipline) and P31 Q17737 (branch of statistics) -- a whole subfield of statistics theory. ProbabilityTheory.posterior (Mathlib/Probability/Kernel/Posterior.lean:70, def posterior (κ : Kernel Ω 𝔏) (μ : Measure Ω) := ((μ ⊗ₘ κ).map Prod.swap).condKernel) is one narrow construction -- the posterior kernel of a prior/likelihood pair -- not a formalization of the discipline. Downgrade to invocation, matching the Q3968/Algebra and Q141495/optimization pattern already in grounding_overrides.jsonl.

[sonnet-collision-14|skeptic:accept] wrong-theorem overclaim: the decl's docstring at Mathlib/Probability/Moments/SubGaussian.lean:786 explicitly labels it '**Hoeffding inequality**' -- it is the Hoeffding tail bound for sums of independent sub-Gaussian variables, not Bernstein's inequality (probability theory), a distinct concentration bound with variance-dependent terms. Grepped Mathlib/Probability for 'Bernstein' (case-insensitive): no dedicated decl exists to reassign to, so downgrade Q826791 from exact/formalization to related rather than replace_decl.

[sonnet-collision-14|skeptic:accept] generalization mismatch, currently tagged 'formalization' (exact-equivalent) in decl_qid_roles_v2.json: Polya enumeration theorem weights orbit-counting by a cycle-index polynomial to count colorings up to symmetry, strictly generalizing plain Burnside orbit-counting. Grepped Mathlib for Polya/cycle-index/CycleIndex (case-insensitive): no dedicated decl exists. The Burnside-lemma decl only formalizes the unweighted special case Polya's theorem specializes to (all-colorings-count-1), so it materially under-formalizes Polya's theorem -- downgrade to special_case (status already correctly 'partial').

[sonnet-collision-14|skeptic:accept] field/object conflation, currently tagged 'formalization' (exact-equivalent) in decl_qid_roles_v2.json: Q1076274 'Choquet theory' carries Wikidata class Q1936384 (field-of-study, same class as linear algebra/Morse theory in the local universe cache) -- it is the discipline studying representation of points in convex sets via extreme points and boundary/representing measures (Choquet simplices, Choquet integral representation), not a single theorem. The decl only proves the compact-convex-set closure(convexHull(extremePoints s)) = s statement, i.e. the classical Krein-Milman theorem -- a foundational precursor result WITHIN Choquet theory, not the theory itself. Grepped Mathlib/Analysis for 'Choquet' (case-insensitive): no hits; no Choquet simplex, representing-measure, or boundary-integral decls exist, and no single folder is scoped to Choquet theory (relevant material is scattered across Analysis/Convex/* and Analysis/LocallyConvex/*), so a folder-level container is not a clean fit either. Downgrade to related, following the Q186247/TangentBundle precedent (object-adjacent mismatch, no reassignable decl or folder).

[sonnet-collision-14|skeptic:accept] generalization mismatch, currently tagged 'formalization' (exact-equivalent) in decl_qid_roles_v2.json despite status already correctly 'partial': Waring's problem asks whether every natural number k has a finite g(k) such that every n is a sum of at most g(k) k-th powers, for ALL k. The decl only proves the k=2 case (g(2)=4, the four-square theorem). Grepped Mathlib for 'Waring' (case-insensitive): no hits, so no decl formalizes the general problem or g(k) for other k. This decl is a genuine, well-known special case of Waring's problem (analogous to the insphere/inscribed-circle legitimate multi-to-multi pattern), not a wrong-decl match -- downgrade match_kind to special_case rather than override the status (already partial) or reassign the decl.

[sonnet-collision-4|skeptic:accept] field/object conflation: Q3174497 'dynamical systems theory' is P31 Q1936384 (field of study) + Q2465832 (branch of science), Wikidata description 'area of mathematics used to describe the behavior of complex dynamical systems, usually by employing differential equations or difference equations' -- this is the DISCIPLINE, not the object Flow (structure Flow ... continuous monoid action, Mathlib/Dynamics/Flow.lean:82). Currently graded special_case/high (same as the object-level Q638328 'dynamical system'); downgrade to invocation to match the sibling discipline-QID pattern already applied elsewhere in this batch and prior batches (Q5498822 'ergodic theory' -> invocation against Ergodic; Q2460595 'transcendental number theory' -> invocation against Transcendental; Q3968 'algebra' -> invocation against Algebra).

[sonnet-collision-5|skeptic:accept] 2026-07-03 audit: field/general-concept overclaim — Q9492 'Probability' is the general notion (measure of expectation an event will occur), currently exact/high against IsProbabilityMeasure; IsProbabilityMeasure is the narrow typeclass 'measure with total mass 1', the technical building block probability theory is built on, not the general concept itself. Sibling QID Q5862903 'Probability theory' on the same decl is already correctly hedged to invocation/medium — align Q9492 to the same pattern (cf. Q3968 Algebra precedent).

[sonnet-collision-5|skeptic:accept] 2026-07-03 audit: object overclaim — Q937378 'logarithmic scale' currently exact/high against Real.logb, but a logarithmic scale is a measurement/display convention (equal distances = equal ratios, e.g. decibels, Richter scale) that USES logarithms, not the logb function itself. Real.logb is the general-base log function; logarithmic scale is a related application, not identical to it. Downgrade exact->related.

[sonnet-collision-6|skeptic:accept] concept adjacency: 'Laplace's equation' (Q339444) is the PDE Δf=0 itself; HarmonicAt is the *property of a function satisfying* that equation (a solution-predicate), not the equation as a mathematical object in its own right — mark special_case (HarmonicAt packages a solution to the equation, plus a regularity condition) rather than treating it as an exact match to the equation

[sonnet-collision-7|skeptic:accept] field/object conflation: Q205084 'computational complexity theory' has P31=Q11862829 (academic discipline), description 'classifies problems according to their inherent difficulty, and relates those classes to each other' -- the field as a whole. TM2ComputableInPolyTime (a per-function poly-time-computability witness structure) is the mechanism defining exactly ONE complexity class (P), analogous to how Group is group theory's foundational object (already downgraded to invocation) and SimpleGraph is graph theory's foundational object (already downgraded to invocation). Currently special_case/medium -- downgrade to invocation to match the established field-vs-foundational-object pattern, since the decl is central to but does not constitute the field.

2026-07-17 Jack (BRAIN v3): `Module` is NOT a generalization of the idea of *vector* — 'these are related but separate concepts'. Both attach options are re-labelled `related` so Vector keeps its own cell instead of being absorbed into the Module atom (generalization outranks special_case, so fixing only Module would silently re-home it into the EuclideanSpace atom, which is worse).
